text: The surgeon applies traction on the ileocolic pedicle to delineate the anatomical planes between the ileocolic pedicle, superior mesenteric artery, and superior mesenteric vein. The dissection focuses on the ileocolic pedicle, with the division of the ileocolic artery occurring near its origin from the superior mesenteric artery.

The surgeon separates the retroperitoneal structures from the mesocolic fascia after dividing the ileocolic pedicle. Dissection proceeds medially towards the C-shaped duodenum and the head of the pancreas, carefully navigating multiple tissue planes.

The surgeon continues the medial to lateral dissection, revealing the fascia of Toldt. The dissection plane is maintained between the fascia of Toldt and the mesocolic fascia, ensuring that the right-sided ureter and gonadal vessels remain protected beneath the fascia layer.

The surgeon dissects and divides the right branch of the middle colic artery at the appropriate level due to the tumor's location in the hepatic fascia. Subsequently, the gastrocolic omentum is opened, with the dissection staying close to the vascular arcade of the stomach.

The surgeon addresses adhesions in the greater omentum and gallbladder, entering the correct dissection plane. The medial to lateral dissection in the infracolic compartment extends to the hepatic flexure, with the gastrocolic omentum opened. The lateral side of the right colon is mobilized, connecting the dissection from the supracolic and infracolic compartments.

The surgeon completes the division of the last strands of the greater omentum at the hepatic flexure, fully mobilizing the right colon. The greater omentum is divided along the proposed resection line of the transverse colon.

The surgeon divides the mesocolon up to the wall of the transverse colon in preparation for resection. The right branch of the middle colic artery is visible on the side of the specimen. After completing the division, the specimen is exteriorized, and an ileocolic anastomosis is performed.
